channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump in his first week back in the White House has offered an early preview to his second-term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's transportation secretary nominee advances to final Senate vote
item.description: The Senate passed a cloture vote on whether to hold a final confirmation vote for Sean Duffy, Trump's nominee to head the Department of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scott Bessent confirmed as treasury secretary, giving him a key role in extending Trump's tax cuts
item.description: The U.S. Senate has confirmed billionaire investor Scott Bessent as President Donald Trump’s treasury secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Texas Republicans in Congress propose bills to codify Trump executive orders
item.description: Ending birthright citizenship, increasing oil drilling, and leaving WHO are just a few bills the state’s Republicans pushed in Congress this month.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Katharine Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: Johnson considering Trump's call to condition California wildfire aid on voter ID crackdown
item.description: House Republicans are at President Trump's Miami-area golf course for their annual issues conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court leaves in place Mississippi’s voting ban for people convicted of some crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday left in place Mississippi’s Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by Indiana sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First lady Melania Trump’s new official portrait revealed
item.description: Melania Trump's new official White House portrait was released on Monday. The first lady is all business in the black and white picture, wearing a suit while striking a power pose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance political advisers join Ramaswamy ahead of expected Ohio governor run
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy is set to launch his bid mid-February, per the source familiar, to succeed term-limited Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine (R) in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Troops from across US headed to southern border
item.description: At least 300 troops landed in El Paso, Texas, over the weekend and at least 500 soldiers are in California.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth's first day
item.description: A flurry of new executive orders from President Donald Trump are expected at the Pentagon as Pete Hegseth, Minnesota native and now defense secretary, began his first day on the job.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Trump addresses House Republicans; Senate confirms Treasury secretary
item.description: President Trump is set to address the House Republicans at their annual policy retreat in Florida, while the Senate votes on the confirmation of his Treasury Department nominee, Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump huddles with House GOP at retreat in Florida
item.description: GOP leaders will try to move Trump’s legislative agenda, including an extension of 2017 tax cuts and border policy.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Graham: Senate needs to investigate Trump decision to pull Bolton’s security
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) says the Senate needs to investigate President Trump’s decision to end his former national security adviser John Bolton’s personal security protection.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, ZEKE MILLER and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Arab allies and confounds a Republican senator
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza fell flat with those countries' governments and left a key congressional ally in Washington perplexed on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump should rethink revoking former officials' security details, Tom Cotton says
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — President Donald Trump should rethink his decision to remove security details from three former senior national security officials, the chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee said Sunday. That protection is not just for them, but also the public, said Sen.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said he would like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “just clean out” the war-torn area to create a virt
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats splintered on immigration and how to respond to Trump
item.description: When asked about Democratic votes for the legislation, the party's leader in the House, New York Rep. Hakeem Jeffries, was circumspect.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's anti-DEI push puts top colleges in the crosshairs
item.description: Universities now find themselves at the frontlines of President Trump's war against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs. An executive order this week instructed federal institutions to investigate DEI programs at schools with endowments of more than $1 billion endowments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump reverses Biden's ban on private prison contracts
item.description: Trump's action comes after signals from private prison corporations like GEO Group and CoreCivic that they expected such a reversal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump touts 'no-tax-on-tips' plan in Nevada victory lap
item.description: When Nevada flipped red in November, Trump became the first GOP presidential candidate to win the key swing state since President George W. Bush in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth is quickly sworn in as defense secretary after dramatic Senate vote
item.description: Pete Hegseth was sworn in Saturday as the nation's 29th secretary of defense, quickly joining President Trump's Cabinet after a dramatic late-night vote in the Senate installed him as the Pentagon's leader.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Loyalty tests and MAGA checks: Inside the Trump White House's intense screening of job-seekers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Job-seekers hoping to join the new Trump administration are facing a series of intense loyalty tests, with White House screening teams fanning out to government agencies to check for “Make America Great Again” bona fides and carefully parsing applicants' politics and social media p
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: This is how Trump organized his Oval Office decor
item.description: Every president gets to decorate the Oval Office to their liking — but sometimes, they keep the decor of their predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
